songs.title: E-E-Egg!
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
E! E! E-e-e!
(eh… eh… eh…)

[Hook]
E! E! E-e-e!
What begins with E?
E-e-egg! E-e-egg!
It's an egg! (an egg!)

[Verse 2]
E! E! E-e-e!
What begins with E?
E-e-elephant! E-e-elephant!
It's an elephant! (an elephant!)

[Verse 3]
E! E! E-e-e!
What begins with E?
E-e-elbow! E-e-elbow!
It's an elbow! (an elbow!)

[Verse 4]
E! E! E-e-e!
What begins with E?
E-e-engine! E-e-engine!
It's an engine! (an engine!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not E!
(eh… eh… eh…)

[Outro]
E-e-egg! E-e-hen!
Ten hens and an egg!
E! E! E-e-e!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: The E sound is the SHORT vowel /e/ as in 'egg' and 'red' — a clipped open 'eh', NOT the letter name 'ee' and NOT the long e of 'be'. Every basket word opens on that short 'eh'. Vowel E lights up the wall this week, so the '(eh… eh… eh…)' drone is the sound of the whole alphabet-vowel row filling in. Answer 'It's an ___!' (an before the vowel) reuses the Week 1 frame.

songs.title: Ten Hens
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(cluck… cluck… cluck…)

[Hook]
Ten hens! Ten hens!
Where are the ten hens?
On the bed! On the bed!
Ten hens on the bed!

[Verse 2]
Ten hens! Ten hens!
Where are the ten hens?
In the pen! In the pen!
Ten hens in the pen!

[Verse 3]
Ten hens! Ten hens!
Where are the ten hens?
On the man! On the man!
Ten hens on the man!

[Verse 4]
Ten hens! Ten hens!
Get the hens! Get the hens!
On the cat?! (on the CAT?!)
Ten hens on the cat!

[Outro]
Ten hens and an egg…
The egg is big…
The egg is a… (PO-TA-TO!)
Ten hens! Ten hens!
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 65-72 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: ONE frame: 'Ten hens on/in the ___!' — the place swaps per verse (bed, pen, man, cat). It doubles as a counting-and-chaos song; keep the (cluck cluck) under the 808. The egg-is-a-potato reveal is the joke — land the PO-TA-TO loud. 'Ten' and 'hen' and 'pen' rhyme on the new short e — lean into them.

spreads.artNote: Ten plump hens lined up, spotlit, dignified — the calm before the chaos. Vowel E lights up the wall in the background.
All ten hens piled onto a small wooden bed, feathers everywhere, absurd. Page-turn escalation begins.
The hens crammed into a tiny wooden pen, overflowing. 'in' vs 'on' quiet contrast.
One bold hen perched on the Dog's head; the Dog utterly resigned. Cast callback.
A flustered man with arms out chasing the scattering hens. Comedy peak.
The hens have turned the tables — all ten roosting on the man, who has given up. The flip.
A single perfect white egg, spotlit in a nest of straw. Sudden quiet.
The egg, now impossibly large, filling the nest. Suspense — that is far too big for a hen.
The 'egg' is the POTATO, sitting in the nest with its tiny round glasses, ten hens staring. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it.

sentences: The hen is on the bed.
extras: 🎉 VOWEL E LIGHTS — light the E on the vowel wall (blue letter, gold star). Sing the five-vowel chant: A · E · I · O · U — four lit, one to go (U in Week 16)
Moveable alphabet: build h-e-n, t-e-n, b-e-d, p-e-t — swap the vowel and hear it change (hat → het? no — pat → pet, bad → bed) to isolate the new short e
Short-vowel discrimination: pat/pet, bad/bed, ban/Ben — minimal pairs that isolate /a/ vs /e/
Ten Hens counting mat: ten hen counters onto ten numbered nests (links to the Ten Little Indians counting tune)

teacherFocus: VOWEL E LIGHTS — the fourth vowel joins the wall (only U remains, in Week 16). Teach the SHORT /e/ of 'egg' and 'hen' (clipped 'eh', never 'ee'), and drill the minimal pairs that separate it from /a/: pat/pet, bad/bed, ban/Ben. Blend h-e-n, t-e-n, b-e-d. The book is pure escalating chaos — ten hens everywhere — so the reading is joyful and the new vowel gets hammered by the -en rhyme (ten, hen, pen). End the week at the vowel wall: four lit, count them.
